channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - wccftech.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for wccftech.com

item.title: Fallout: New Vegas Remaster Seemingly Teased by Iron Galaxy
item.content:encoded:
• 0-20%: Unlikely - Lacks credible sources
  • 21-40%: Questionable - Some concerns remain
  • 41-60%: Plausible - Reasonable evidence
  • 61-80%: Probable - Strong evidence
  • 81-100%: Highly Likely - Multiple reliable sources

We might finally have a solid clue of who's working on the long-rumored Fallout: New Vegas remaster: it's Iron Galaxy, which has dropped a pretty heavy-handed tease on LinkedIn: a "Please Stand By" picture from a Fallout game with the background of a bright desert environment and the following accompanying statement:

Today’s our February company meeting. It’s time to catch up with what the company’s been up to and what’s coming up next for IG.

The Chicago-based game developer, which is very fond of its independence, has released a few original titles in the past, including Wreckateer, Divekick, Extinction, and Rumbleverse. However, it's mostly known for its work on porting, co-development, and remakes.

That said, it's a bit of a hit-and-miss track record. We've got great examples of remakes and ports, like the recent Tony Hawk’s Pro Skater 3+4 release, 2023's Metroid Prime Remastered, and their solid work on bringing The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim to Nintendo Switch and The Elder Scrolls Online to PlayStation 4 and Xbox One.

On the other hand, there are at least two major PC disasters that bear Iron Galaxy's mark: Batman: Arkham Knight and The Last of Us Part I. The former was so broken that publisher Warner Bros. Interactive Entertainment had to suspend PC sales for a while until the main game developer, Rocksteady, could fix it through patches. The port of Naughty Dog's masterpiece was also troubled by various bugs, memory leaks, and stutters, among other issues.

Both ports were eventually improved and now enjoy positive reviews on Steam, but their existence does raise a yellow flag for the Fallout: New Vegas remaster. That said, Bethesda probably chose them because Iron Galaxy's work on their games was well-received overall.

We've known of a Fallout 3 remaster for a long time (originally thanks to Microsoft "leaking" Bethesda's internal schedule during the FTC proceedings to finalize the acquisition of Activision Blizzard), but recently, we started hearing from WindowsCentral that Fallout: New Vegas should also be in the works. Bethesda's Todd Howard did say the studio is working on "a number of Fallout projects", after all. New Vegas is the most critically acclaimed installment and was also recently featured in the second season of the Amazon Prime Video show, so it's an obvious candidate for a remaster.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/fallout-new-vegas-remaster-seemingly-teased-iron-galaxy/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: CAPCOM Spotlight Returns on March 5 with PRAGMATA, Monster Hunter Stories 3 and More
item.content:encoded:
Today, Japanese developer and publisher CAPCOM announced that a new episode of its CAPCOM Spotlight series of livestreams will air on Thursday, March 5, at 2 PM Pacific Time.

This CAPCOM Spotlight will last approximately half an hour, focusing on the following upcoming and existing games:

  • Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection
  • PRAGMATA
  • Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection
  • Mega Man Dual Override
  • Street Fighter 6

Below, we've added a brief overview of each featured game, in release date order.

Street Fighter 6 is the only game that's already out. Launched in June 2023 to critical acclaim, it marked the studio's return to form for its flagship fighting franchise after the rocky reception to Street Fighter V. It introduced a new control scheme called Modern Controls to lower the barrier to entry for newcomers, a fully fleshed-out single-player World Tour mode, and a Battle Hub online space for players to gather and compete. The game has been regularly updated with new fighters and content, further cementing its status as one of the most successful entries in the series' history. In all likelihood, the CAPCOM Spotlight will feature a new DLC fighter announcement.

Monster Hunter Stories 3: Twisted Reflection, the third entry in the spin-off RPG series set in the Monster Hunter universe, follows the two rival kingdoms of Azuria and Vermeil as they struggle to survive an environmental disaster known as the Crystal Encroachment. The players control the heir to Azuria, who teams up with Vermeil's princess, Eleanor, to combat the threat while navigating the fraught politics between the two nations. Like its predecessors, the game centres on becoming a Rider who bonds with and battles alongside monsters, using a turn-based combat system. It will be released on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, Nintendo Switch 2, and PC via Steam on March 13, 2026.

Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection is a compilation of all three entries in the Mega Man Star Force series, originally released for the Nintendo DS between 2006 and 2008, now emulated and enhanced for modern platforms. All seven versions of the three games are included, alongside new difficulty and assist options, an autosave function, adjustable encounter rates, and fully rearranged soundtracks alongside the originals. Online card trading and battles are also supported, and the collection is scheduled for release on March 27, 2026.

PRAGMATA, on the other hand, is CAPCOM's long-awaited new sci-fi IP. It's an action-adventure game set on a lunar research station, following spacefarer Hugh and android Diana as they work together to fight the hostile AI controlling the station and attempt to return to Earth. X The game blends third-person gunplay with a unique real-time hacking mechanic: Diana uses her hacking capabilities to disable enemy armour while Hugh takes them down with firearms. Originally announced back in 2020 and delayed multiple times, it finally received a concrete release date at The Game Awards 2025: April 24, 2026. This means it's less than two months away from its debut.

Mega Man: Dual Override is the twelfth mainline entry in the classic Mega Man series, following Mega Man 11 from 2018. Also announced at The Game Awards 2025, the game reportedly takes a classic approach to the Blue Bomber (run, shoot, slide, and battle colorful, dangerous robots) while featuring a refreshed visual style. Alongside the announcement, CAPCOM launched a Robot Master design contest, giving fans the chance to submit ideas for a boss character to appear in the game; this is a tradition that goes back to earlier entries in the series. The game is set for 2027, the same year the Mega Man series celebrates its 40th anniversary.

Which of these games are you most excited to see at the imminent CAPCOM Spotlight?

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/capcom-spotlight-returns-march-5-pragmata-monster-hunter-stories-3/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
